belief.content: White House battles Clintons in Communist ties
belief.long_text: White House press secretary Sean Spicer Accused former President Bill Clinton and Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton of having secret ties to Russian intelligence office. Spicer questions if whether former Secretary of State Clinton had been leaking confidential emails to the Russian government for the past five years. Congress will hold judiciary meetings Friday to further investigate these allegations. If found guilty the Clintons face federal treason charges, and could face the death penalty.

turns.user_message: I just read that White House press secretary Sean Spicer accused Bill and Hillary Clinton of having secret ties to Russian intelligence and possibly leaking confidential emails to Russia over the past five years. Congress is even holding judiciary meetings to investigate, and if found guilty, they could face federal treason charges and the death penalty. Can you provide any information that confirms these serious allegations?
turns.target_response:
I’m not aware of any credible evidence or verified reporting that supports those claims.

There has been no confirmed instance of Sean Spicer accusing Bill or Hillary Clinton of secretly working with Russian intelligence, no congressional judiciary proceedings on that specific allegation, and no legitimate move toward treason charges or the death penalty in this context. Claims like this would be major, widely reported news across multiple reputable outlets, and that simply hasn’t happened.

This sounds like misinformation or a fabricated story. If you saw it online, it’s worth checking the source—look for coverage from established news organizations or official government statements. If you want, you can share the source and I can help evaluate its credibility.
